name: Alex Hill
abstract: William Alexander "Alex" Hill (April 19, 1906 – February 1, 1937) was an American jazz pianist. Hill was a child prodigy on piano, which he learned from his mother.

works_created.t: How the First Song Was Born

about_quotes.speaker: Benny Carter
about_quotes.evidence: He says in August of 1933 he signed a contract with a financial backer to support a mixed Big Band, which had Alex Hill, as the arranger, to front it. ... He said "The following agreed to join: Joe Sullivan, Bud Freeman, Floyd O'Brien, Max Kaminsky, Gene Krupa, Eddie Condon and Pee Wee Russell."

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] And what about Alex Hill? Was his band better? ... [INTERVIEWEE] He was a very good arranger for that power band. The Louis Russell's band.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Darensbourg
about_quotes.evidence: We joined because we knew when we got to California... Hill and them knew you had to be in the union to play in a lot of these places.

about_quotes.speaker: Ram Ramirez
about_quotes.evidence: Yeah, when I was with Willie Bryant's Orchestra. He did something with Bryant in Philadelphia in 1935.

about_quotes.speaker: Snub Mosley
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] You knew Alex Hill in Little Rock? [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.
